information_boundary: Only information reasonably available by 09:30 ET is included. Same-day outcome and post-open catalysts are excluded.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The session was primarily a relief and positioning rebound. The index recovered from a compressed, oversold five-day decline as AI leadership re-engaged. Geopolitical risk remained present, but stable yields prevented it from overwhelming the equity rebound.
main_drivers: Broad risk rebound after five straight S&P 500 losses.
AI and chip shares rebounded strongly, helped by large announced South Korean chip-investment plans.
Oil rose but Treasury yields were comparatively stable, allowing growth stocks to recover.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The close of the quarter extended the AI rebound. Index strength was concentrated in semiconductors and AI infrastructure rather than a clean all-sector expansion. Easing oil helped risk appetite, while higher yields limited the breadth of the move.
main_drivers: Semiconductors and AI infrastructure led another advance.
Oil eased while Treasury yields rose, producing a growth-positive but not fully broad risk-on tape.
Quarter-end buying helped trim June losses.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: This was a divergence day: underlying breadth was healthier than the index, but concentrated technology and memory-chip selling controlled the cap-weighted benchmarks. Softer manufacturing reduced rate pressure but was insufficient to offset valuation-driven tech liquidation.
main_drivers: Weakness in influential technology and memory-chip stocks outweighed positive breadth.
A softer-than-expected manufacturing reading helped Treasury yields retreat from their highs.
Most S&P sectors rose, but concentrated megacap weakness kept SPY/S&P 500 slightly negative.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The macro impulse was dovish, but the index response was fragmented. Rate-sensitive and non-tech shares rallied on weak payroll growth, while crowded AI/chip positions continued to unwind. SPY therefore behaved as a tug-of-war between broad easing-sensitive strength and concentrated technology weakness.
main_drivers: Payroll growth came in much weaker than expected, reducing expectations for a near-term Fed hike.
Dow and broad-market shares benefited from lower-rate expectations.
Semiconductors sold off sharply for a second day, leaving the S&P 500 flat and Nasdaq lower despite positive breadth.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The session was a narrow AI-led rebound rather than broad risk-on confirmation. Falling oil and yields created room for growth shares to recover, but weak breadth meant the move depended heavily on megacap and semiconductor leadership.
main_drivers: Broadcom and other AI names rebounded, lifting the Nasdaq and S&P 500.
The majority of S&P 500 stocks still fell, showing narrow leadership.
Easing oil and yields reduced macro pressure.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The market absorbed a direct inflation/geopolitical shock. Higher oil and yields compressed growth multiples and intensified semiconductor selling. The S&P 500 held up better than tech-heavy indexes because capital rotated rather than exiting equities uniformly.
main_drivers: The U.S. Treasury revoked an Iranian-oil sales waiver, increasing supply and shipping risk.
WTI crude surged, Treasury yields rose on inflation concerns, and the dollar firmed.
Chipmakers fell sharply; rotation into other sectors limited the S&P 500 decline.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: Geopolitical headline risk overrode the attempted stabilization. The S&P 500 declined because renewed war escalation raised oil, inflation and duration uncertainty. Selected chip rebounds prevented a more uniform technology collapse, but the session's dominant causal force was the breakdown of the diplomatic narrative.
main_drivers: President Trump said the interim Iran deal was 'over' and warned of additional strikes.
Oil and geopolitical risk increased while the Dow and S&P weakened.
Broadcom and selected battered chip shares rebounded, creating mixed index internals.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: This was a partial reversal of the prior day's geopolitical shock. Falling oil and yields relaxed the inflation channel, allowing growth leadership to reassert itself. The S&P 500 more than recovered the prior loss as the market treated escalation risk as less immediate.
main_drivers: Oil gave back much of the prior day's surge as markets calmed.
Treasury yields eased, reducing pressure on risk assets.
AI and growth shares recovered, lifting the Nasdaq and S&P 500.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The session extended the AI-risk bid but with less force than the prior day. Semiconductor optimism and lower oil supported SPY, while weak small-cap participation showed that the move remained leadership-dependent rather than universally risk-on.
main_drivers: AI and semiconductor appetite remained strong.
SK Hynix surged in its U.S. debut, reinforcing AI-infrastructure enthusiasm.
Oil eased and volatility fell, supporting a modest index advance, while small caps lagged.

prior_days_context.causal_summary: The session combined two bearish channels: an oil-driven inflation shock and renewed liquidation in crowded AI leadership. The oil surge raised yields and reduced the market's willingness to pay elevated growth multiples. SPY declined as both macro and index concentration worked in the same direction.
main_drivers: Brent crude surged nearly 10% as U.S.-Iran tensions and shipping-control claims intensified.
Treasury yields rose on renewed inflation concerns.
AI and semiconductor shares fell sharply, with Micron and Nvidia among the main index drags.

items: The prior session had delivered an oil and AI-led selloff.
June CPI was the primary scheduled catalyst and had the ability to offset or reinforce war-driven inflation fears.
Major U.S. banks were reporting earnings; oil remained elevated because U.S.-Iran escalation risk persisted.
same_day_premarket.starting_prior: Use as a probabilistic prior and require live market transmission.

research_integrity: No same-day post-09:30 catalyst or closing result is included.
